description.en: Finland's everyday celebration bread: a golden braid perfumed with fresh-ground cardamom, brushed with egg and topped with crunchy pearl sugar. Central to the kahvipöytä coffee table, from Sunday afternoons to Christmas and name-day parties.
description.de: Finnlands alltägliches Festtagsbrot: ein goldener Zopf, parfümiert mit frisch gemahlenem Kardamom, mit Ei bestrichen und mit knackigem Hagelzucker bestreut. Herzstück des Kaffeetischs (kahvipöytä), vom Sonntagnachmittag bis Weihnachten und Namenstagen.
description.es: El pan festivo cotidiano de Finlandia: una trenza dorada perfumada con cardamomo recién molido, pincelada con huevo y cubierta de azúcar perlado crujiente. Protagonista de la mesa de café (kahvipöytä), de las tardes de domingo a la Navidad.
description.fr: Le pain de fête du quotidien en Finlande : une tresse dorée parfumée à la cardamome fraîchement moulue, dorée à l'œuf et couverte de sucre perlé croquant. Star de la table à café (kahvipöytä), du dimanche après-midi à Noël.

origin.note: Also called nisu, an older term still used in Finnish-American and Finnish-Canadian communities. Cardamom reached Finland via Viking-era trade routes through Constantinople, becoming the country's signature sweet-bread spice.

ingredients.note: freshly ground from whole pods for best aroma
pearl sugar for topping, does not melt when baked
for egg wash across both loaves, brushed before baking; not counted in hydration

keeping.note: Wrap well once fully cool; slightly stale pulla toasts beautifully or makes excellent French toast.

method.title: Bloom and mix
method.summary: Warm the milk, stir in the yeast, then combine with flour, sugar, salt, egg, butter and freshly ground cardamom into a soft dough.

method.title: Knead
method.summary: Work the dough until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes by hand or with a mixer.

method.title: Bulk rise
method.summary: Cover and let rise until doubled, roughly 1.5 hours.

method.title: Divide and braid
method.summary: Split the dough into portions, roll each piece into ropes, and braid them into loaves.

method.title: Second rise
method.summary: Let the shaped loaves proof, uncovered or lightly covered, until puffy, about an hour.

method.title: Finish and bake
method.summary: Brush with egg wash, scatter generously with pearl sugar, and bake until deeply golden and fragrant.

source.adaptation: Kept as the source's full 2-loaf batch. Pearl-sugar topping and egg wash excluded from hydration/sugar % since they're not part of the fermented dough itself.
